Raw response excerpt: To solve the problem of reconstructing a valid sequence of sibling-merge operations, we must first understand the nature of the operation. A sibling-merge operation takes two adjacent siblings in a tree and merges them into a single node. This operation preserves the parent-child relationship structure but reduces the number of children of a specific parent.
